title: Bartlettina constipatiflora
intro:
There is limited information available about the plant Bartlettina constipatiflora. However, I can provide general information about the plant family it belongs to. 

Bartlettina is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The plant species in this genus are native to the Americas and are known for their medicinal properties. 

Some species of Bartlettina, including Bartlettina constipatiflora, are traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of these plants include the reduction of inflammation and the treatment of infections.

The bioactive constituents of Bartlettina constipatiflora, like other plants in the Asteraceae family, include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.

Bartlettina constipatiflora is not commonly used in herbal preparations, but some species of the Bartlettina genus are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for their medicinal properties. These preparations are not widely available or commonly used in modern medicine.
